message.content: Slate a pace at the end of BB to go through the changes made during BB and determine what RBS0 updates are needed to keep sync.  Don't do the work now though, too soon.

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: pristine-walkthrough-manual (₢BBAAc) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Operator-driven manual walkthrough from a marshal-zeroed state, positioned between canonical-establish (built) and canonical-onboarding-sequence (yet to be built). Two purposes under the gauntlet design:
  
  1. Validate canonical-establish in actually-fresh marshal-zero state — its precondition probes were designed defensively, but only running against a real fresh state surfaces silent first-build assumptions in the underlying tabtargets.
  2. Inform canonical-onboarding-sequence case design — walking §3 manually surfaces the case shape, probe specifications, and operator-facing diagnostic ceremony for the next fixture's cases.
  
  Cognitive posture: real-time conversation, surprise-by-surprise. Operator drives commands; agent watches output, reads code on demand, captures dispositions.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  
  §2 (canonical-establish run as a fixture against marshal-zero), §3 (reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain, walked manually), and §4 (crucible suite per RELEASE.md) each completed end-to-end against a fresh marshal-zero. Every surprise captured + dispositioned (inline fix or surfaced as follow-up pace before canonical-onboarding-sequence mounts). Fixed-or-surfaced is sufficient — green tally is BBAAX's guardrail, not this pace's.
  
  ## Cost
  
  ~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt. Same magnitude as BBAAX, distinct purpose: this pace's bug surface is decomposed step-by-step (manual operator drive) and informs probe design before the next fixture exists; BBAAX validates the fully-stitched suite end-to-end.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Per BBAAN posture: invest is fatal-on-exists; divest deletes production RBRA; unmake absorbs governor cleanup. Any retry path goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery.
  - Reuse existing tabtargets exclusively; canonical-establish runs as a fixture; §3 and §4 walked manually.
  - No code changes to canonical-establish during this pace (it's already built). Surprises in canonical-establish surface as follow-up paces.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - gauntlet-canonical-establish landed (the §2 fixture exists for run-as-fixture step).
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - §1 (pristine-lifecycle exercises it; not relevant here).
  - Canonical-onboarding-sequence implementation (subsequent pace, informed by this walkthrough's findings).
  - Suite assembly (subsequent pace).
  - Green-tally end-to-end run (BBAAX).
  - Fixing every surprise inline — large or design-call surprises surface as follow-up paces.

0957bb03  ₢BBAAp    Move JWT-propagation race handling from producer-side probe into consumer-side retry inside rbgo_get_token_capture, so every RBRA consumer absorbs the post-write `invalid_grant` + `Invalid JWT Signature` window rather than gating only the mint sites. Producers (RBGG SA-key creation, RBGP governor mantle) drop their explicit probe calls; RBGC constants and RBGO stderr file rename track the new locality.                                                                                                                                                                                                    
ab32cabc  ₢BBAAo    RBRA install-advice locality sweep across all three SA-credential producers. Governor mantle (rbgp_Payor.sh): RBRA write target moved from BURD_OUTPUT_DIR/governor-${timestamp}.rbra to RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE — matches the singleton-assay-slot discipline Director/Retriever already honor; decoded JSON also relocated from BURD_TEMP_DIR to the assay subdirectory (mirrors zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key's 'credentials never leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR' pattern). The 'or, for assay (test) mode' branch in install advice deleted (vacuous after the change — file is already at assay). BBAAn's JWT-bearer-probe call site is unchanged at the signature level; ${z_rbra_file} now equals RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE so the probe operates on the new target naturally. All three install-advice blocks (Governor + Director + Retriever) switch from cp to mv per the volatile-singleton-staging semantics: assay is transient; install means relocate, not duplicate. Director/Retriever advice reframes around the mint-on-behalf flow — 'Move the RBRA file to a safe place — typically delivered to the operator who consumes this identity. When the invester is also the consumer, the local role slot is the canonical destination'. Governor advice prescribes RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE (operator self-mints, self-uses). RBS0:803-812 (rbsk_invest_serialized linked term): Governor added to the singleton-assay-writer enumeration alongside Retriever and Director; verb corrected throughout (copy → move); 'safe place' framing reflected; rationale extended with 'Move (not copy) prevents the assay slot from accumulating stale credential bytes after promotion'. canonical-establish fixture (rbtdrk_canonical.rs::rbtdrk_governor_mantle_impl): replaced the BURV-output glob lookup (looking for governor-*.rbra in mantle.burv_output) with the same shape the D/R invest cases use — resolve assay path before mantle, run mantle, assert assay.exists() after mantle (the wrap-criterion-6 locality probe), copy assay→canonical governor slot. Dropped governor-rbra-src locating logic; assay slot is the contract surface now. Verification: bash -n clean on rbgp_Payor.sh and rbgg_Governor.sh; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean; tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 86/86 pass. Out of scope per docket: retroactive cleanup of credential bytes left in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by prior runs; spec doc touches beyond RBS0:803-812 (RBSGM/RBSDK/RBSRK install-ceremony detail); delivery-channel patterns for 'safe place' advice.              
a0de51ec  ₢BBAAn    Fix duplicate role-prefix in JWT-bearer probe diagnostic label. Both call sites prepended the role name to an account-name string that already starts with the role: rbgg passed '${z_role}-${z_account_name}' where z_account_name = '${z_role}-${z_identity}', producing labels like 'retriever-retriever-foo' / 'director-director-foo'; rbgp passed '${RBCC_role_governor}-${z_governor_account_id}' where z_governor_account_id = '${RBCC_role_governor}-${z_timestamp}', producing 'governor-governor-202604281016'. Surfaced by live rbw-aM run output. Functionally harmless (label is operator-facing diagnostic only, not on the wrap-criterion path), but the doubled prefix misleads on timeout messages — fixed inline by passing the already-prefixed account-name verbatim. bash -n clean on both files.

All three install-advice blocks (Governor + Director + Retriever) switch from cp to mv per the volatile-singleton-staging semantics: assay is transient; install means relocate, not duplicate. Director/Retriever advice reframes around the mint-on-behalf flow — 'Move the RBRA file to a safe place — typically delivered to the operator who consumes this identity. When the invester is also the consumer, the local role slot is the canonical destination'. Governor advice prescribes RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE (operator self-mints, self-uses). RBS0:803-812 (rbsk_invest_serialized linked term): Governor added to the singleton-assay-writer enumeration alongside Retriever and Director; verb corrected throughout (copy → move); 'safe place' framing reflected; rationale extended with 'Move (not copy) prevents the assay slot from accumulating stale credential bytes after promotion'. canonical-establish fixture (rbtdrk_canonical.rs::rbtdrk_governor_mantle_impl): replaced the BURV-output glob lookup (looking for governor-*.rbra in mantle.burv_output) with the same shape the D/R invest cases use — resolve assay path before mantle, run mantle, assert assay.exists() after mantle (the wrap-criterion-6 locality probe), copy assay→canonical governor slot. Dropped governor-rbra-src locating logic; assay slot is the contract surface now. Verification: bash -n clean on rbgp_Payor.sh and rbgg_Governor.sh; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean; tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 86/86 pass. Out of scope per docket: retroactive cleanup of credential bytes left in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by prior runs; spec doc touches beyond RBS0:803-812 (RBSGM/RBSDK/RBSRK install-ceremony detail); delivery-channel patterns for 'safe place' advice.              
a0de51ec  ₢BBAAn    Fix duplicate role-prefix in JWT-bearer probe diagnostic label. Both call sites prepended the role name to an account-name string that already starts with the role: rbgg passed '${z_role}-${z_account_name}' where z_account_name = '${z_role}-${z_identity}', producing labels like 'retriever-retriever-foo' / 'director-director-foo'; rbgp passed '${RBCC_role_governor}-${z_governor_account_id}' where z_governor_account_id = '${RBCC_role_governor}-${z_timestamp}', producing 'governor-governor-202604281016'. Surfaced by live rbw-aM run output. Functionally harmless (label is operator-facing diagnostic only, not on the wrap-criterion path), but the doubled prefix misleads on timeout messages — fixed inline by passing the already-prefixed account-name verbatim. bash -n clean on both files.

attachment.prompt: Please explain what took you > 4m to do this?  Seemed like it could be done without any research at all.  ?
over 5m now.  I need to understand why you went away for so long!

lastPrompt: Slate a pace at the end of BB to go through the changes made during BB and determine what RBS0 updates are needed to keep sync.  Don't do the work now though, too soon.

content.text: You're right — this should have been quick. Let me explain after I get this slated.

input.content:
# jjezs_slate rbs0-sync-sweep-from-bb-changes

## Character

Reflective audit pace at the tail of BB. Walk the changes landed across BB's paces and determine which RBS0 (and adjacent veiled-spec) sections fell out of sync with code/behavior, then update them.

Cognitive posture: deliberate read-and-compare. Source of truth is the code/handbook surface as it stands at mount-time; RBS0 is being reconciled to it.

## Wrap criterion

RBS0 (and any directly-implicated sibling spec files under `rbk/vov_veiled/`) reflect the behavior shipped during BB. Each touched section either updated or explicitly judged still-correct. No silent drift left behind for the next heat.

## Constraints

- Read-only against code; spec edits only.
- Scope is BB's changes — not a full RBS0 audit.

## Out of scope

- Restructuring RBS0 beyond what BB's changes require.
- Spec touches outside the veiled-spec family unless directly implicated.

## Character

content.text:
Slated as ₢BBAAq at end of BB.

**Why it took so long — honest answer:** I called `jjx_orient` before slating, which I didn't need. `jjx_enroll` defaults to end-of-heat, so all I needed was: open officium → write gazette → enroll. The orient call returned a massive payload (full pace list, file-touch bitmap, swim lanes, recent-work commit messages) that I then had to process before getting to the actual slate. That was wasted work — you told me explicitly "don't do the work now," and orient is research I'd only need if I were preparing to mount the pace.

The minimum-viable path was three calls. I did four, and the extra one was the expensive one.

message.content: Ah.  Hmm, help me decide if we ought to work out a simplification of text from orient.  Thoughts?

content.text: My read: the bug was protocol, not payload — orient is the *mount* entry point and I called it for a *slate*, which only needs `jjx_enroll`. Trimming orient's output would help the wrong axis: the bitmap/swim-lanes are genuinely useful when you're actually mounting, and shrinking them invites a follow-up call to recover context. I'd patch CLAUDE.md's Quick Verbs row instead — make explicit that "slate at end of heat" is `jjx_enroll` directly, no orient — rather than reshape orient itself.

message.content: Hmm, is there a simple jjk*.md amendment that would help?  "Hard cases make bad law" and I don't know how much to try and optimize this particular case.

content.text: Honestly, probably not worth it. The slate→`jjx_enroll` mapping is already in the Quick Verbs table; what failed was my discipline, not the doc. A one-line "orient is only for mount" rule would fit, but it patches a single slip and starts a slope toward verb-by-verb annotations. I'd let this one sit and only amend if the pattern repeats.
